findings_details.title: vi imported after vi.mock() calls — wrong ordering
findings_details.body: Lines 20-29 call vi.mock() and vi.mock() before vi is imported on line 31. While vitest hoists vi.mock() calls, the named import of `vi` on line 31 placed after the mock calls is misleading and breaks linting/type-check ordering expectations. The standard pattern is to import vi at the top alongside other vitest imports (line 11 already imports describe/it/expect from vitest but omits vi). The mock calls at lines 20-29 should move below the imports, or `vi` should be added to the line-11 import. As written any linter or reader sees `vi` used before it is imported.

findings_details.title: No afterEach/afterAll cleanup — temp directories leak
findings_details.body: setupRepo() creates a temp directory via mkdtempSync() on every test invocation (line 34), but there is no afterEach or afterAll that removes them. The branch-cleanup-error-messages.test.ts file in contrast has a proper afterEach that drains a tempDirs array. The 5 tests in prune-e2e.test.ts leave orphaned directories under the OS temp dir on every test run. Fix: add an afterEach (or afterAll) that collects and rmSync({recursive:true,force:true}) every dir created by setupRepo().

findings_details.title: execSync (shell form) used where execFileSync is the project pattern
findings_details.body: The sibling test file branch-cleanup-error-messages.test.ts uses execFileSync throughout to avoid shell injection risk and stay consistent with branch-cleanup.ts. prune-e2e.test.ts uses execSync (shell form) for all git calls. The arguments are static strings so injection is not a live risk here, but inconsistency with the established pattern makes the test code harder to maintain and slightly more fragile if branch names are ever interpolated. Fix: replace execSync git invocations with execFileSync using an argv array, matching the style in branch-cleanup-error-messages.test.ts.

findings_details.title: results list hidden on 'done' rows — diagnostic output silently discarded
findings_details.body: showResults on line 119 gates display with `rowState.status === 'failed'`. The server (worktree-prune-batch-runner.ts:281) sets row.results on BOTH done and failed rows — results contains items like 'Stopped 2 terminals', 'Stopped preview server', 'Removed worktree', 'Deleted local branch'. For failed rows the git output is shown; for done rows it is silently discarded. This means if a partial failure occurs (e.g., 'Could not stop terminals' appears in results but no critical-failure prefix fires, so status=done), the operator never sees it. The fix: show results on done rows too, or at minimum show results on done rows when they contain any 'Could not ...' line.

findings_details.title: addWorktree uses shell-form execSync with interpolated branch name
findings_details.body: Line 48: `execSync(\`git worktree add -b \"${branch}\" \"${worktreePath}\"\`...)`. Unlike the static git calls, this one interpolates `branch` and `worktreePath` into a shell string. In tests branch names are caller-controlled so injection is not a live risk, but this is still the wrong pattern. execFileSync with an argv array eliminates the quoting complexity and the double-escaping that is already visible in this line.
